jobs.title: Marketing Coordinator - Austin West Riverside
jobs.description:
For more than 20 years, AEG has played a pivotal role in transforming sports and live entertainment. Annually, we host more than 160 million guests, promote more than 10,000 shows and present more than 22,000 events around the world. We are committed to innovation, artistry, and community, and leverage the power of our 300+ venues, leading sports franchises, marquee music brands, integrated entertainment districts, premier ticketing platform and global sponsorship activations, to create memorable moments that give the world reason to cheer.

Our business is interwoven with the human mind and heart, and we strive to build a diverse and inclusive company that reflects the artists, athletes, and fans that we host; reach beyond traditional boundaries to support the communities in which we operate; and minimize our impact on the environment by adopting sustainable practices throughout our business operations.

If you want to be challenged to up your game and make a difference, then join us in giving the world reason to cheer!

A Brief Overview
The Marketing Coordinator is responsible for marketing, social media, online e-marketing public relations, promotions for venues, radio promotions, clubs, theaters, festivals, shows and one-offs. This position will develop promotional initiatives with artists and the community, to promote positive brand image. The Marketing Coordinator may also be responsible for coordinating the work of interns and street team for show promotions.

Essential Functions:

  • Monitor marketing trends, regionally, with artists, and implement new marketing strategies and campaigns based on current trends. Analyze fan base to target specific demographics to promote shows, tours and festivals.
  • Helps coordinate and advance day-of-show activities including radio stations set ups, remote broadcasts and escorting media photographers and news crews.
  • Creates, proof reads and distributes weekly newsletters. Order and deploy e-cards and e-blasts with AXS Advantage tool. Check that venue information and links are correct, communicate errors to designers, check for accuracy, communicate errors and sign off on final version.
  • Develop and manage social media accounts, research new promotional activities and prizes for online social media giveaways. Collaborate with artists to promote shows on their social media accounts. Design appropriate posting schedule that corresponds with various event related deadlines: announce, on sale, maintenance and backend.
  • Create and deliver all show settlements including actuals, backup and any special campaign notes.
  • Collect ticket links and keep central grid, check all links, pre-sale passwords, billing, art on ticket pages and venue websites
  • Coordinate asset trafficking for digital media campaigns – admats, banners, videos etc.
  • Maintain good working relationships with partners, co-promoters and media. Develop special initiatives to involve the community in events. Organize artist promotions and meet and greets.
  • May also be responsible for the upload of expense reports, updating of office calendar, ordering of supplies and general office administration.

Required Qualifications:

  • High School Diploma or its equivalency
  • 2-4 years Of related work experience
  • Experience calendaring events
  • Knowledgeable and skilled in MS Office (Excel, Word, Outlook and Photoshop)
  • Basic accounting knowledge
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • Strong PR skills, social media savvy, and organization skills
  • Must have strong work ethic, problem solving and prioritization skills
  • Must be able to work independently and as part of a team
  • Creative thinker; thinks “outside the box”
  • Knowledge of the music industry preferred

Payscale: $17.97 - $26.44

Bonus: This position is not eligible for a bonus under the current bonus plan requirements.

Benefits: Full-time: We offer a comprehensive benefits package that includes: medical, dental and vision insurance, paid holidays, vacation and sick time, company paid basic life insurance, voluntary life insurance, parental leave, 401k Plan (with a current employer match of 3%), flexible spending and health savings account options, and wellness offerings.

AEG reserves the right to change or modify the employee’s job description whether orally or in writing, at any time during the employment relationship.  AEG may require an employee to perform duties outside their normal description.

AEG's policy is to hire the most qualified applicants, and we comply with all applicable federal, state and local employment laws in making hiring and employee decisions.  We are an equal opportunity employer and do not discriminate against applicants or employees on the basis of race, color, marital status, disability, religion, age, sex, sexual orientation, national origin, genetic information, veteran status, or any other legally protected status recognized by applicable federal, state or local law.

Employer does not offer work visa sponsorship for this position.

jobs.title: Content Creator (Latin Music) - AWAL
jobs.description:
At Sony Music Entertainment, we fuel the creative journey. We’ve played a pioneering role in music history, from the first-ever music label to the invention of the flat disc record. We’ve nurtured some of music’s most iconic artists and produced some of the most influential recordings of all time.

Today, we work in more than 70 countries, supporting a diverse roster of international superstars, developing and independent artists, and visionary creators. From our position at the intersection of music, entertainment, and technology, we bring imagination and expertise to the newest products and platforms, embrace new business models, employ breakthrough tools, and provide powerful insights that help our artists push creative boundaries and reach new audiences. In everything we do, we’re committed to artistic integrity, transparency, and entrepreneurship.

Sony Music Entertainment is a member of the Sony family of global companies.

The Content Creator will be a key member of our digital marketing team, working across the full AWAL catalogue of artists with a special focus on creating content for Latin region and other diaspora communities within the US.  Your main objective is to generate interest and ultimately streams for our artists, doing so by creating and distributing engaging and sticky content on social media and driving dialogue about your content and the music connected to it.  You must be deeply familiar with Latin music and other global music scenes to succeed in this role. You will have the flexibility to identify the trends you are most excited about, create the content you think will most resonate, and pick the music from AWAL that is the best fit. You will be responsible for strategizing on the best ways and places to distribute that content and for executing on that strategy. In a nutshell, we are looking for someone who is already creating and distributing content through socials, deeply passionate about building a career in music, self-driven in executing strategies that support our artists, and globally minded. Anyone who can join our team and deliver on that mission will find unlimited opportunities for growth at AWAL.

What you'll do:

  • You live immersed in culture, particularly for Latin communities in the US
  • You speak Spanish fluently or natively
  • You are intensely curious and highly creative
  • You create your own path
  • You have experience with content creation, design, editing and copywriting
  • You are chronically online and tapped into current social media conversations & internet trends
  • You are hungry and hard working and as interested in team goals as your own
  • You understand the difference between something real and something fleeting
  • You are proactive and a self-starter
  • You are motivated to make an immediate and tangible impact with your work
  • You want to see your work lead to real artists building real audiences
  • You want to shape the future of music marketing vs following strategies from the past
  • You want a place where an accelerated career in music is within reach

Who you are:

You have previously built and grown social accounts and have independently created content that has achieved virality. Alongside your application, please submit links to the accounts you have created and operated containing the content you have shot/produced/designed/edited.

What we give you:

  • A temporary contract
  • You join an inclusive, collaborative and global community where you have the opportunity to channel your passion every day
  • A modern office environment designed to foster productivity, creativity, and teamwork empowering you to bring your best

Sony Music is committed to providing equal employment opportunity for all persons regardless of age, disability, national origin, race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity or expression, pregnancy, veteran or military status, marital and civil partnership/union status, alienage or citizenship status, creed, genetic information or any other status protected by applicable federal, state, or local law.

California based applicants have certain rights under California law. Please see our California Recruitment Privacy Policy for more details.

The anticipated annual base salary does not include any other compensation components or other benefits that an individual may be eligible for.  The actual base salary offered depends on a variety of factors, which may include as applicable, the qualifications of the individual applicant for the position, years of relevant experience, specific and unique skills, level of education attained, certifications or other professional licenses held, and the location in which the applicant lives and/or from which they will be performing the job.

jobs.title: Fall Intern, Publicity - Part - Time
jobs.description:
Who We Are: 
 SiriusXM and its brands (Pandora, SiriusXM Media, AdsWizz, Simplecast, and SiriusXM   Connect) are leading a new era of audio entertainment and services by delivering the most   compelling subscription and ad-supported audio entertainment experience for listeners -- in the   car, at home, and anywhere on the go with connected devices. Our vision is to shape the future   of audio, where everyone can be efforlessly connected to the voices, stories and music they   love wherever they are. 
 This is the place where a diverse group of emerging talent and legends alike come to share   authentic and purposeful songs, stories, sounds and insights through some of the best   programming and technology in the world. Our critically-acclaimed, industry-leading audio   entertainment encompasses music, sports, comedy, news, talk, live events, and podcasting. No   matter their individual role, each of our employees plays a vital part in bringing SiriusXM’s vision   to life every day. 
 
 SiriusXM   is the leading audio entertainment company in North America, and the premier   programmer and platform for subscription and digital advertising-supported audio products.   SiriusXM’s platforms collectively reach approximately 150 million listeners, the largest digital   audio audience across paid and free tiers in North America, and deliver music, sports, talk,   news, comedy, entertainment and podcasts. Pandora, a subsidiary of SiriusXM, is the largest   ad-supported audio entertainment streaming service in the U.S. SiriusXM's subsidiaries   Simplecast and AdsWizz make it a leader in podcast hosting, production, distribution, analytics   and monetization. The Company’s advertising sales organization, which operates as SiriusXM   Media, leverages its scale, cross-platform sales organization and ad tech capabilities to deliver   results for audio creators and advertisers. SiriusXM, through SiriusXM Canada Holdings, Inc.,   also offers satellite radio and audio entertainment in Canada. In addition to its audio   entertainment businesses, SiriusXM offers connected vehicle sevices to automakers. 
 
 How you'll make an impact: 
SiriusXM is seeking a Publicity Intern interested in gaining hands-on experience supporting a dynamic Communications team. This role offers the opportunity to learn how strategic communications and media relations help shape the stories behind one of the world's leading audio entertainment companies.
The Communications team supports a broad portfolio of content and business initiatives across music, entertainment, comedy, podcasting, sports and news. While the internship will provide exposure across all of these categories, we're looking for someone with a particular passion and focus on sports and news and an interest in the media landscape surrounding both.

 What you'll do: 
 Assist the Publicity team with day-to-day communications initiatives and media relations efforts focused heavily on sports, news and talk programming.
 Monitor and track media coverage across print, broadcast, digital and social platforms.
 Compile regular media coverage reports, press recaps and communications metrics.
 Conduct research on media outlets, journalists, industry trends and competitive activity.
 Help develop and maintain targeted media lists and editorial calendars.
 Assist with drafting and proofreading press materials, including press releases, media alerts, and reporter pitches.
 Assist with media events, interviews, press announcements and other communications activities.
 Participate in team meetings, brainstorming sessions and cross-functional collaboration.
 Perform other duties as required.
 
 What you'll need: 
 Internships are open to current students enrolled at a university (MBA students graduating by September 2027 are preferred) and recent graduates who graduated within the 12-month period prior to the start of the internship
 Currently pursuing a bachelor's or master's degree in Communications, Public Relations, Journalism, Marketing, English, Broadcasting, or a related field.
 Excellent written and verbal communication skills with strong attention to detail.
 Strong organizational skills and the ability to manage multiple projects in a fast-paced environment.
 Interest in media, storytelling, and the evolving communications landscape.
 Passion for sports and news, with curiosity about the media, personalities, and stories shaping both the sports and political industries.
 Familiarity with Microsoft Office and Google Workspace; experience with media monitoring or communications tools such as Muck Rack and Critical Mention is a plus.
 A collaborative attitude, strong work ethic, and eagerness to learn.
 Experience with automation and/or AI tools such as chatGPT, Gemini
 Must have legal right to work in the U.S.
 
 At SiriusXM, we carefully consider a wide range of factors when determining compensation, including your background and experience. These considerations can cause your compensation to vary. We expect the base salary for this position to be $18/hr and will depend on your skills, qualifications, and experience. Additionally, this role might be eligible for discretionary short-term and long-term incentives. We encourage all interested candidates to apply.

SiriusXM and its brands (Pandora, SXM Media, AdsWizz, Simplecast, and SiriusXM Connect) are leading a new era of audio entertainment and services by delivering the most compelling subscription and ad-supported audio entertainment experience for listeners -- in the car, at home, and anywhere on the go with connected devices. Our vision is to shape the future of audio, where everyone can be effortlessly connected to the voices, stories and music they love wherever they are.
This is the place where a diverse group of emerging talent and legends alike come to share authentic and purposeful songs, stories, sounds and insights through some of the best programming and technology in the world. Our critically-acclaimed, industry-leading audio entertainment encompasses music, sports, comedy, news, talk, live events, and podcasting. No matter their individual role, each of our employees plays a vital part in bringing SiriusXM’s vision to life every day.

 SiriusXM 
SiriusXM is the leading audio entertainment company in North America, and the premier programmer and platform for subscription and digital advertising-supported audio products. SiriusXM’s platforms collectively reach approximately 150 million listeners, the largest digital audio audience across paid and free tiers in North America, and deliver music, sports, talk, news, comedy, entertainment and podcasts. Pandora, a subsidiary of SiriusXM, is the largest ad-supported audio entertainment streaming service in the U.S. SiriusXM's subsidiaries Simplecast and AdsWizz make it a leader in podcast hosting, production, distribution, analytics and monetization. The Company’s advertising sales organization, which operates as SXM Media, leverages its scale, cross-platform sales organization and ad tech capabilities to deliver results for audio creators and advertisers. SiriusXM, through Sirius XM Canada Holdings, Inc., also offers satellite radio and audio entertainment in Canada. In addition to its audio entertainment businesses, SiriusXM offers connected vehicle services to automakers.

 How you’ll make an impact: 
SiriusXM is looking for an intern to work cross-functionally with our programming teams to provide content marketing support for our music, entertainment, and sports verticals.

 What you’ll do: 
 Familiarize yourself with partner brand guidelines and content.
 Brainstorm creative concepts to help drive engagement across socials and marketing outlets.
 Draft marketing proposals to be shared with external partners.
 Monitor and track contractual obligations for all partners across music, talk, and sports.
 Generate PowerPoint presentations recapping for numerous initiatives including show and channel launches, content campaigns, and events.
 Assist coordinators with circulating approved assets and programming details to other lines of business using Monday.com.
 Assist with administrative duties such as booking conference rooms, scheduling meetings, and submitting invoices.
 
 What you’ll need: 
 Internships are open to current students enrolled at a university (those graduating by September 2027 are preferred) and recent graduates who graduated within the 12-month period prior to the start of the internship as well as broadcasting schools with one-year programs that provide certificates of completion.
 Thorough knowledge of MS-Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Access). PowerPoint is a must.
 Basic photo editing experience (i.e. photoshop and canva) is a plus.
 Marketing, advertising, media or communications majors preferred.
 Passion for music, entertainment, and sports is a must.
 Good project management and planning skills.
 Interpersonal skills and ability to interact and work with staff at all levels.
 Excellent written and oral communication skills.
 Attention to detail skills.
 Ability to handle multiple tasks in a fast-paced environment.
 Willingness to take initiative and to follow through on projects.
 Must have legal right to work in the U.S.
 
At SiriusXM, we carefully consider a wide range of factors when determining compensation, including your background and experience. These considerations can cause your compensation to vary. We expect the base salary for this position to be $18/hr and will depend on your skills, qualifications, and experience. Additionally, this role might be eligible for discretionary short-term and long-term incentives. We encourage all interested candidates to apply.

Our goal at SiriusXM is to provide and maintain a work environment that fosters mutual respect, professionalism and cooperation. jobs.title: Senior Director, Creator Strategy & Viral Marketing - AWAL
jobs.description:
About Sony Music Entertainment

At Sony Music Entertainment, we fuel the creative journey. We’ve played a pioneering role in music history, from the first-ever music label to the invention of the flat disc record. We’ve nurtured some of music’s most iconic artists and produced some of the most influential recordings of all time.

Today, we work in more than 70 countries, supporting a diverse roster of international superstars, developing and independent artists, and visionary creators. From our position at the intersection of music, entertainment, and technology, we bring imagination and expertise to the newest products and platforms, embrace new business models, employ breakthrough tools, and provide powerful insights that help our artists push creative boundaries and reach new audiences. In everything we do, we’re committed to artistic integrity, transparency, and entrepreneurship.

Sony Music Entertainment is a member of the Sony family of global companies.

As Senior Director, Creator Strategy & Viral Marketing at AWAL, you will lead the label's US creator ecosystem and oversee our digital content team to turn real-time internet trends, algorithmic patterns, and high-impact creator partnerships into scalable viral moments that drive global streaming consumption.

What you'll do:

  • Lead influencer and creator strategy across AWAL’s roster, building a scalable network of relationships with influencers/creators, agencies and managers to deliver high-impact campaigns across priority releases
  • Act as our internal creator marketing & niche community expert across all genres and target audiences, driving thought leadership across the organization
  • Track and analyze fast-moving digital trends, internet culture, and algorithmic behaviors to ensure continuous relevance for AWAL artists.
  • Drive creator campaigns from ideation through execution, handling outreach and budget negotiations to maximize efficiency, impact and ROI
  • Track and analyze campaign KPIs to inform next steps & identify new opportunities for growth across target audiences
  • Deliver weekly trend analysis and innovative content strategies that keep our music at the epicenter of Gen Z conversations
  • Oversee the Viral Content team's daily operations and weekly priorities while maintaining a comprehensive cultural calendar to capitalize on real-time trends & timely events
  • Establish internal processes to scale top-performing content across the roster, maximizing KPIs, streaming metrics, and TikTok/Reels sound engagement
  • Collaborate with Audience Development and Viral Content teams to conceptualize and execute innovative, multi-platform initiatives, pushing boundaries to translate big-picture concepts into scalable audience acquisition and sustained viral growth

Who you are:

  • 6+ years of experience in influencer or creator marketing within the music or entertainment industry
  • Passionate about music with an expert understanding of pop culture, digital trends, and fan behavior
  • Experience leading teams and managing junior team members
  • Strong relationship-building skills with an established network of influencers, creators, external agencies, and talent managers
  • Experience executing end-to-end creator marketing campaigns, from sourcing and briefing to tracking, reporting, and analysis
  • Strategic thinker with a deep understanding of digital platform algorithms and niche online communities
  • Expert at translating fast-moving internet trends and fan behavior into high-impact short-form content strategies
  • Excellent organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple complex campaigns simultaneously in a fast-paced environment
 

What we give you:

  • You join an inclusive, collaborative and global community where you have the opportunity to channel your passion every day  
  • A modern office environment designed to foster productivity, creativity, and teamwork empowering you to bring your best 
  • An attractive and comprehensive benefits package including medical, dental, vision, life & disability coverage, and 401K + employer matching 
  • Voluntary benefits like company-paid identity theft protection and resources for pets, mental health and meditation resources, industry-leading fertility coverage, fully paid leave for childbirth or bonding, fully paid leave for caregivers, programs for loved ones with developmental disabilities and neurodiversity, subsidized back-up child and elder care, and reimbursement for adoption, surrogacy, tuition, and student loans 
  • Investment in your professional growth and development enabling you to thrive in our vibrant community.  
  • The space to accelerate progress, positively disrupt, and create what happens next  
  • Time off for a winter recess

Sony Music is committed to providing equal employment opportunity for all persons regardless of age, disability, national origin, race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity or expression, pregnancy, veteran or military status, marital and civil partnership/union status, alienage or citizenship status, creed, genetic information or any other status protected by applicable federal, state, or local law.

The anticipated annual base salary does not include any other compensation components or other benefits that an individual may be eligible for.  The actual base salary offered depends on a variety of factors, which may include as applicable, the qualifications of the individual applicant for the position, years of relevant experience, specific and unique skills, level of education attained, certifications or other professional licenses held, and the location in which the applicant lives and/or from which they will be performing the job.

 This is the place where a diverse group of emerging talent and legends alike come to share authentic and purposeful songs, stories, sounds, and insights through some of the best programming and technology in the world.  How You’ll Make an Impact 
 As Senior Manager of Internal Communications, you will play a critical role in shaping how information educates, inspires, and engages team members across the organization. You are passionate about developing and delivering messages that help our colleagues understand what we’re doing, why it matters, and the impact it has on our business.
 
 In this role, you will leverage multiple communication channels to increase awareness of key company initiatives, strengthen our corporate culture, and enhance the overall team member experience. You’ll partner closely with senior leaders across the business to create compelling communications that inform the workforce, celebrate achievements, and showcase the many facets of life at SiriusXM.
 
 You are a strategic thought leader and captivating storyteller who brings people together through clear, purposeful messaging. With strong attention to detail and a highly organized, versatile approach, you will successfully support a wide breadth of communications—driving alignment, enabling change, and fostering a more connected and engaged workplace.
 
 What You’ll Do 
 Own and drive the internal communications strategy for a broad portfolio of business functions, including, but not limited to, Social Impact, Facilities, Safety & Security, Finance, Technology, Strategy, and Marketing—aligning with enterprise goals and defining how key initiatives are communicated across the organization.

 Partner with and advise senior leaders and cross-functional stakeholders, shaping communications approaches for high-impact initiatives and translating complex work into clear, compelling messaging that drives awareness, understanding, and action.

 Define and drive messaging standards across departments and business units, establishing best practices for voice, tone, and narrative.

 Set and manage communications planning cadence and editorial prioritization, determining how and when key messages are delivered across channels to maximize reach and impact.

 Lead the development and execution of strategic communications plans that highlight business impact and strengthen team member connection to company priorities.

 Oversee the strategy and execution of the company-wide newsletter, preparing content to celebrate achievements, showcase key initiatives, and reinforce culture.

 Collaborate with Corporate Communications, Public Relations, and Employer Brand to align internal and external messaging and amplify Programming news and industry recognition internally.

 Report on and analyze internal communications metrics, including engagement, sentiment, and channel performance, leveraging insights to continuously refine communications strategies and improve outcomes.

 Manage internal communications processes and operating rhythms, driving efficiency and scalability across planning and execution.

 Provide guidance and mentorship to cross-functional partners, helping elevate communications effectiveness across the organization.

 
 What You’ll Need 
 Bachelor's degree in business, communications, journalism, or related field, or equivalent, relevant experience.
 8+ years of experience in internal communications or related field.
 Proven ability to develop and execute strategic communications plans that support diverse business functions and enterprise-wide initiatives.
 Exceptional writing, editing, and storytelling skills, with the ability to translate complex topics into clear, engaging content.
 Ability to maintain consistency in voice, tone, and messaging across a wide range of topics and business areas.
 Experience partnering with and advising senior leaders and cross-functional stakeholders.
 Strong organizational skills, with an execution-oriented mindset and the ability to manage multiple priorities and stakeholders simultaneously.
 Demonstrated interpersonal and relationship-building skills, with experience effectively engaging, influencing, and establishing trust with stakeholders at all levels of the organization.
 Highly collaborative, adaptable, and detail-oriented, with a proactive and solutions-focused approach.

jobs.title: Digital Marketing Manager (m/w/d) - Family Entertainment
jobs.description:
Vollzeit, ab 01.09.2026, unbefristet, Standort München

Du möchtest Audio-Streaming nicht nur operativ steuern, sondern strukturiert weiterentwickeln? Du interessierst dich für Plattformlogiken, Performance, SEO und Automatisierung – und packst gleichzeitig gerne selbst mit an, wenn es um Profilpflege, Playlists und Releases geht?

Und du hast Lust, mit starken Marken im Family Entertainment zu arbeiten?
Dann bist du bei uns genau richtig.

 

Deine Rolle

In dieser Position verantwortest du das Streaming- und Plattformmanagement für ein vielfältiges Portfolio im Bereich Hörspiel und Kindermusik.

Zu Beginn ist die Rolle bewusst operativ geprägt: Du stellst eine exzellente Umsetzung in der Profilpflege, im Playlist-Management sowie im Pitching und in der Platzierung unserer Releases sicher.

Gleichzeitig entwickelst du die Funktion perspektivisch weiter – hin zu einer datengetriebenen, skalierbaren und automatisierten Steuerung unserer Streaming-Aktivitäten, um die Performance nachhaltig zu wachsen.

 

Deine Aufgaben

  • Steuerung des Streaming- und Plattformmanagements für unsere Hörspiel- und Kindermusik-Releases über alle relevanten DSPs (u.  Spotify, Apple Music, Amazon Music)
  • Operative Pflege und kontinuierliche Optimierung von Artist-Profilen, Artist Picks, Verlinkungen und redaktionellen Präsentationsflächen
  • Kuratierung und Weiterentwicklung unserer Playlist-Strukturen (Neuheiten, thematische Playlists, Cross-Promotion)
  • Redaktionelles und technisches Pitching aller Releases sowie Identifikation zusätzlicher Platzierungspotenziale
  • Kontinuierliches Monitoring von Placements, Playlist-Features und Performance mit Ableitung konkreter Maßnahmen
  • Aufbau und Weiterentwicklung datengetriebener Steuerungslogiken (Monitoring, Reporting, Dashboards)
  • Optimierung von Discovery- und SEO-Potenzialen sowie Konzeption und Durchführung strukturierter Tests (z.  Titel, Cover, Playlist-Logiken)
  • Identifikation von Automatisierungspotenzialen sowie kontinuierliches Plattform- und Marktmonitoring
  • Enge Zusammenarbeit mit Teams aus Marketing, Digital Sales sowie Data & Tech

 

Dein Profil

  • Abgeschlossenes Studium im Bereich Marketing, Medien, Wirtschaft, Data, Digital Business oder eine vergleichbare Qualifikation
  • Mehrjährige relevante Berufserfahrung (mind. 3 Jahre) im digitalen Marketing, Streaming, Plattformmanagement oder Performance-Umfeld (Branchenhintergrund ist ein Plus, aber kein Muss)
  • Grundverständnis für Streaming-Plattformen und Playlist-Logiken – idealerweise auch aus eigener Nutzung
  • Stark ausgeprägtes analytisches Denkvermögen sowie die Fähigkeit, datenbasierte Erkenntnisse in konkrete Maßnahmen zu übersetzen
  • Erste Erfahrung mit SEO- und Discovery-orientierten Ansätzen sowie ein gutes Verständnis für Plattform- und Suchlogiken
  • Hohe Tool- und Tech-Affinität sowie Interesse an Daten, Automatisierung und Skalierung (z.  BI-Tools wie Tableau)
  • Strukturierte, lösungsorientierte Arbeitsweise mit ausgeprägter Testing-Mentalität
  • Fähigkeit, sowohl operativ tief einzusteigen als auch strategisch zu denken
  • Hands-on-Mentalität und hohe Sorgfalt bei operativen Aufgaben
  • Kommunikationsstärke und Freude an der Zusammenarbeit mit verschiedenen Schnittstellen

 

Wir bieten dir

  • Raum für Vielfalt und für Innovation.
  • Ein dynamisches Arbeitsumfeld mit hoher Projektvielfalt
  • Die Freiheit, Prozesse und Methoden zu challengen und zu revolutionieren
  • Die Möglichkeit, an starken Marken im Family Entertainment aktiv mitzuwirken und diese weiterzuentwickeln (u.  Die drei ???, Peppa Pig)
  • Ein Team, das Verantwortung übernimmt, kollaborativ arbeitet und Themen aktiv vorantreibt
  • Gestaltungsspielraum, um Strukturen, Prozesse und Performance nachhaltig zu verbessern
  • Ein Zuschuss zum Deutschland-Ticket, flexible Arbeitszeiten und Remote Work.
  • Betriebliche Altersvorsorge. Ein Streaming-Abo deiner Wahl.
  • Konzerte in ganz Deutschland und Events in unserem Office im Campus Neue Balan.
  • Exklusive Trainings in der hauseigenen Sony Music Academy.
  • Sowie das Vertrauen, deine Projekte in Eigenregie zum Erfolg zu führen.

 

Wie du dich bewirbst

Dein Foto, Alter, Familienstand, deine Herkunft, Zeugnisse, die Jobs deiner Eltern und andere Merkmale benötigen wir nicht.

Zeige uns in einem CV oder einer anderen kreativen Form, was dich ausmacht, was du kannst und warum Sony Music mit dir besser klingt!

Wir freuen uns auf dich.
Sony Music sounds better with you

Sony Music Entertainment GmbH

jobs.title: Creative Marketing Design Specialist
jobs.description:
Company Information
For more than 20 years, AEG has played a pivotal role in transforming sports and live entertainment. Annually, we host more than 160 million guests, promote more than 10,000 shows and present more than 22,000 events around the world. We are committed to innovation, artistry, and community, and leverage the power of our 300+ venues, leading sports franchises, marquee music brands, integrated entertainment districts, premier ticketing platform and global sponsorship activations, to create memorable moments that give the world reason to cheer.

Our business is interwoven with the human mind and heart, and we strive to build a diverse and inclusive company that reflects the artists, athletes, and fans that we host; reach beyond traditional boundaries to support the communities in which we operate; and minimize our impact on the environment by adopting sustainable practices throughout our business operations.

If you want to be challenged to up your game and make a difference, then join us in giving the world reason to cheer!

Job Summary

The Creative Marketing Design Specialist plays a pivotal role within the Creative team, serving as a key liaison for the sales, activation, events and performance optimization departments. This role is responsible for managing and executing multiple moderately-complex projects simultaneously while ensuring alignment with organizational goals and branding standards. With a strong focus on collaboration, the Creative Marketing Design Specialist works seamlessly across departments to deliver innovative and high-quality creative materials.

Essential Functions

  • Designs compelling recap and marketing presentations using tools such as Figma, InDesign, Ceros, Photoshop, and Illustrator for all AEG business units. Collaborates closely with the Sales and Activation teams to understand strategic partnership goals and translate them into visually engaging materials.
  • Determines content needs by gathering, organizing, and presenting information in a clear and concise manner, while ensuring the design aligns with AEG and Global Partnerships (GP) brand standards.
  • Leads and follows best practices in the recap development process, ensuring timely and effective execution of innovative materials that support Global Partnerships’ business objectives.
  • Supports brand-building initiatives by developing and maintaining branded templates, graphics, and design elements used across all GP marketing and sales platforms.
  • Partners with the Data Analytics team to brainstorm, design, and deliver custom charts, graphs, and data visualizations that enhance storytelling within recaps, proposals and presentations.
  • Participates in bi-weekly Global Partnerships Creative Team meetings and collaborates with creative teams across AEG’s divisions to share and present new design concepts, ensuring alignment with AEG-wide creative standards and sharing best practices.
  • Manages the Global Partnerships asset library—including sales, activation, and internal resources—by regularly updating and organizing assets, maintaining version control, and ensuring accessibility for internal and external use. Provides training and ongoing support to Sales and Activation teams on how to effectively use these materials. Proactively updates collateral to reflect changes in business needs, ensuring that all materials remain current, relevant, and aligned with branding guidelines.

 

Required Qualifications

  • BA/BS Degree (4-year) Graphic Design, Visual Communications or a related area
  • 2-4 years Related experience
  • Experience with Adobe InDesign & MS Office Suite (Word, Excel, Outlook)
  • Experience with Graphic Design: posters, logos, & infographics, Photography, Video Production, & Web Design

This role will manage multiple budgets at once and place media that optimizes for right message, right time, right consumer to drive ticket sales. Book and account for all advertising and media ensuring funds are allocated to correct partners. Oversee advertising settlements for each show.

  • Create email marketing campaigns and edit content to ensure a high open rates, CTR and conversions.  Manage Content Management System (CMS) which supports the publishing of web content as needed.
  • Oversee creation of graphics for ecards, banners, posters, social media post, radio station needs and emails. Oversee creation of graphics for ecards, banners, posters, social media posts, radio station needs and emails.
  • Conduct marketing research and compile businesses for promotions based on fan interest/demographics including influencers.
  • Build and maintain relationships with local businesses for promotional opportunities.  Collaborate with agencies and vendor partners like Ticketmaster and AXS on marketing opportunities for shows, emails, banners and promotions.

jobs.title: College Marketing Representative, Nashville - The Orchard
jobs.description:
About The Orchard

The Orchard is a leading global music distributor and Artist & Label services company operating in 50+ markets worldwide. The Orchard empowers creators and businesses to grow and adapt in the dynamic global music industry. We specialize in being artist focused, blending industry expertise and technology, leveraging data to drive success, and providing streamlined digital and physical distribution solutions. The Orchard’s comprehensive artist & label services offerings include marketing, sync licensing, premium video services, transparent data analysis, advertising, rights management, radio promotion, D2C, and more.

The Orchard focuses on providing a comfortable, social, and engaging environment to encourage productivity and creativity.

As a paid part‑time employee of The Orchard, you’ll serve as a key advocate for the artists we distribute. Each month, you’ll promote 10–20 artists through a mix of digital and in‑person marketing across your campus and college town. You’ll be a tastemaker in your community, collaborating with student organizations, local businesses, record stores, DJs, promoters, and more to build meaningful visibility for new releases and touring artists across a wide range of genres.

In this role, you’ll lead listening events, organize campus activations, and create social media content that directly supports our artists. You’ll gain hands-on experience in music marketing while staying ahead of the curve on emerging talent and new music, all while shaping how students in your market discover and engage with The Orchard’s roster.

  • Attend our artists' shows to hand out promotional tools and get feedback from fans

  • You have the opportunity to express your creativity through your locally run social channels, in our team brainstorms, and in your concert/album reviews
  • You’ll have access to inside information that will improve your marketing outreach
  • You’ll receive free ticket(s) into our artists’ shows & possible meet and greet passes
  • You’ll build valuable relationships within your local music industry

Who you are:

  • You have an understanding of social media marketing, you stay current on trends, and you have an active presence creating content on TikTok, Instagram, X, and YouTube
  • You have availability of 20 hours per week
  • You’re well-written, well-spoken, a team player, and an excellent communicator

jobs.title: Senior Director, Strategic Marketing (Brand Partnerships) - AWAL
jobs.description:
About Sony Music Entertainment

At Sony Music Entertainment, we fuel the creative journey. We’ve played a pioneering role in music history, from the first-ever music label to the invention of the flat disc record. We’ve nurtured some of music’s most iconic artists and produced some of the most influential recordings of all time.

Today, we work in more than 70 countries, supporting a diverse roster of international superstars, developing and independent artists, and visionary creators. From our position at the intersection of music, entertainment, and technology, we bring imagination and expertise to the newest products and platforms, embrace new business models, employ breakthrough tools, and provide powerful insights that help our artists push creative boundaries and reach new audiences. In everything we do, we’re committed to artistic integrity, transparency, and entrepreneurship.

Sony Music Entertainment is a member of the Sony family of global companies.

AWAL is seeking a dynamic and passionate Senior Director of Strategic Marketing to lead the development, negotiation, and execution of innovative brand partnership opportunities across AWAL’s artist roster. This role will serve as a key connector between artists, managers, brands, agencies and internal teams to create culturally relevant campaigns that drive revenue, audience growth, and long-term artist brand value.

The ideal candidate is a highly connected business development leader with deep experience in music, entertainment, lifestyle marketing, and integrated partnerships. This individual should possess strong negotiation skills, a passion for artist development, and a proven ability to create meaningful collaborations between brands and culture-driving talent.

What you'll do:

Brand Partnership Strategy 

  • Develop and execute AWAL’s overall brand partnerships strategy in alignment with company growth objectives and artist priorities.
  • Identify, source, and secure new partnership opportunities across categories including fashion, technology, gaming, automotive, beverage, consumer goods, wellness, sports, and social platforms.
  • Generate sponsorship, endorsement, experiential, content, and integrated marketing opportunities for AWAL artists and campaigns.
  • Build custom partnership proposals and revenue-generating programs tailored to artists, releases, tours and cultural moments.

 

Relationship Management

  • Cultivate and maintain senior-level relationships with brands, agencies, media partners,  marketing executives, philanthropic organizations and other strategic partners.
  • Serve as the primary point of contact for brand-related opportunities across AWAL’s roster.
  • Work closely with artist managers, attorneys, and internal stakeholders to ensure alignment on partnership execution.

Campaign Execution

  • Lead end-to-end partnership execution from ideation and negotiation through activation and reporting
  • Collaborate with marketing, creative, digital, sync and publicity teams to execute integrated campaigns.
  • Ensure all partnerships authentically align with artist brands, audiences, and long-term career strategy.
  • Oversee timelines, budgets, approvals, contractual obligations, and performance metrics.

Market Insights & Innovation

  • Stay ahead of emerging trends across music, creator economy, social media, live experiences, and brand marketing.
  • Identify innovative formats and nontraditional partnership opportunities that position AWAL as a leader in artist-brand collaboration.
  • Use data and audience insights to inform partnership strategy and campaign effectiveness.

Who you are:

  • 8–10+ years of experience in brand partnerships, strategic marketing, business development, sponsorships or integrated marketing within music, entertainment, media, sports, or agency environments.
  • Proven track record of securing and executing high-value partnerships and sponsorship deals.
  • Deep understanding of music culture, artist marketing, digital platforms, and the brand/entertainment ecosystems.
  • Strong network of brand and agency relationships.
  • Exceptional communication, presentation, negotiation, and relationship management skills.
  • Ability to work cross-functionally in a fast-paced, entrepreneurial environment.
  • Strong strategic thinking and commercial acumen.
  • Experience working directly with artists, managers, and talent teams preferred.
  • Passion for independent music and artist development.

 

What we give you:

  • You join an inclusive, collaborative and global community where you have the opportunity to channel your passion every day  
  • A modern office environment designed to foster productivity, creativity, and teamwork empowering you to bring your best 
  • An attractive and comprehensive benefits package including medical, dental, vision, life & disability coverage, and 401K + employer matching 
  • Voluntary benefits like company-paid identity theft protection and resources for pets, mental health and meditation resources, industry-leading fertility coverage, fully paid leave for childbirth or bonding, fully paid leave for caregivers, programs for loved ones with developmental disabilities and neurodiversity, subsidized back-up child and elder care, and reimbursement for adoption, surrogacy, tuition, and student loans 
  • Investment in your professional growth and development enabling you to thrive in our vibrant community.  
  • The space to accelerate progress, positively disrupt, and create what happens next  
  • Time off for a winter recess

Sony Music is committed to providing equal employment opportunity for all persons regardless of age, disability, national origin, race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity or expression, pregnancy, veteran or military status, marital and civil partnership/union status, alienage or citizenship status, creed, genetic information or any other status protected by applicable federal, state, or local law.

jobs.description:
At Warner Music Group, we’re a global collective of music makers and music lovers, tech innovators and inspired entrepreneurs, game-changing creatives and passionate team members. Here, we turn dreams into stardom and audiences into fans. We are guided by three core values that underpin everything we do across all our diverse businesses: 
 
• Curiosity: We do our best work when we’re immersing ourselves in culture and breaking through barriers. Curiosity is the driving force behind creativity and ingenuity. It fuels innovation, and innovation is the key to our future. 
 
• Collaboration: Making music and bringing it to the world is all about the power of originality amplified by teamwork. A great idea, like a great song, travels globally. We ignite passions and build connections across our diverse community of artists, songwriters, partners, and fans.
 
• Commitment: We pursue excellence for our team and our talent. Everything in music starts with a leap into the unknown, and we’re committed to keeping the faith, acting with integrity, and delivering on our promises.
 
Technology is one of the most important parts of our business. Whether it’s supporting our songwriters, ensuring we provide the right royalty data to global partners, or helping our creative teams use the latest AI tools to discover new talent—technology plays an invaluable role in our success. Product Managers help turn vision into reality. Check out first-hand accounts from the WMG engineering, product, design, infrastructure and data teams in our blog!
 
WMG is home to a wide range of artists, musicians, and songwriters that fuel our success. That is why we are committed to creating a work environment that actively values, appreciates, and respects everyone. We encourage applications from people with a wide variety of backgrounds and experiences.
 
Consider a career at WMG and get the best of both worlds – an innovative global music company that retains the creative spirit of a nimble independent.


The Role:
Technology is one of the most important parts of our business. Whether it’s supporting our songwriters, ensuring we provide the right royalty data to global partners, or helping our creative teams use the latest AI tools to discover new talent—technology plays an invaluable role in our success. Product Managers help turn vision into reality.
Inspired by successful programs for developing new product leaders, our Associate Product Manager (APM) role is designed to train and mentor the next generation of product talent. This role is focused on Marketing & Growth Automation. This team is managing the underlying technology for how WMG markets its vast catalog, leveraging data, AI, and automation to achieve massive scale and efficiency. 
As an APM, you’ll have the opportunity to build a portfolio of features and products aimed at growing our catalog market share, developing scalable tools for marketing teams, and increasing engagement across platforms like Spotify, Apple Music, and social media.
If you’re a recent graduate who thrives in a dynamic environment and is excited about creating impactful technology for music creators, we’d love to hear from you!

About Sony Music Entertainment

At Sony Music Entertainment, we fuel the creative journey. We’ve played a pioneering role in music history, from the first-ever music label to the invention of the flat disc record. We’ve nurtured some of music’s most iconic artists and produced some of the most influential recordings of all time.

From our position at the intersection of music, entertainment, and technology, we bring imagination and expertise to the newest products and platforms, embrace new business models, employ breakthrough tools, and provide powerful insights that help our artists push creative boundaries and reach new audiences. jobs.title: College Marketing Representative, Atlanta - The Orchard
jobs.description:
About The Orchard

The Orchard is a leading full-service music distribution company operating in 48 markets worldwide and servicing top global independent artists and labels. With cutting-edge operations and an unparalleled global team, The Orchard partners with clients to provide full scale solutions across a variety of verticals. Championed by an exceptional community of music lovers and experts, The Orchard empowers creators to grow and evolve in the dynamic, global industry.  

  

The Orchard focuses on providing a comfortable, social, and engaging environment to encourage productivity and creativity. 

As a paid part‑time employee of The Orchard, you’ll serve as a key advocate for the artists we distribute. Each month, you’ll promote 10–20 artists through a mix of digital and in‑person marketing across your campus and college town. You’ll be a tastemaker in your community, collaborating with student organizations, local businesses, record stores, DJs, promoters, and more to build meaningful visibility for new releases and touring artists across a wide range of genres.

In this role, you’ll lead listening events, organize campus activations, and create social media content that directly supports our artists. You’ll gain hands-on experience in music marketing while staying ahead of the curve on emerging talent and new music, all while shaping how students in your market discover and engage with The Orchard’s roster.

What you'll do:

  • Organically create awareness of The Orchard’s artists by cultivating authentic relationships with key campus and local influencers including college radio, student organizations, newspapers, and community media to unlock opportunities for reviews, radio plays, interviews, and standout exposure.
  • Maintain a local social media presence that promotes the artists you’ll be working with, utilizing a mix of digital assets that are supplied to you and creating your own content to support The Orchard’s artists
  • Implement in-person marketing initiatives at record stores, lifestyle shops, and on campus
  • Research appropriate online sites, forums, and networks for the key 18-24yr old demographic for each artist
  • Research & attend local and campus events that will best facilitate the promotion of our artists
  • Attend our artists' shows to hand out promotional tools and get feedback from fans
  • Submit a detailed report on your marketing successes and initiatives for each campaign you run

Perks:

  • You will gain real world music industry experience that will be a great addition to your resume
  • You have the opportunity to express your creativity through your locally run social channels, in our team brainstorms, and in your concert/album reviews
  • You’ll have access to inside information that will improve your marketing outreach
  • You’ll receive free ticket(s) into our artists’ shows & possible meet and greet passes
  • You’ll build valuable relationships within your local music industry
  • + many more!

Who you are:

  • You are an Undergraduate Student currently enrolled in a 4 year university in Atlanta with a minimum of 2 years left prior to graduation
  • You are able to manage your own hours and be accountable for the work assigned to you
  • You have a passion for music, an understanding of how your peers discover and consume music, and a deep interest in music business & marketing
  • You have an understanding of social media marketing, you stay current on trends, and you have an active presence creating content on TikTok, Instagram, X, and YouTube
  • You have availability of 20 hours per week
  • You’re well-written, well-spoken, a team player, and an excellent communicator
  • You have transportation methods and live within 30 minutes of Atlanta
  • You have a flexible schedule for team conference calls and tour coverage

What We Give You

  • You join an inclusive, collaborative and global community where you have the opportunity to channel your passion every day  
  • A modern office environment designed to foster productivity, creativity, and teamwork empowering you to bring your best 
  • Opportunities to connect with our valuable resources, such as our Counsel of Equity & Progressive Action (CEPA), a group created by our employees tasked with developing and implementing innovative solutions to advance a globally-shared goal of ensuring fair and inclusive spaces for all
  • An attractive and comprehensive benefits package including medical, dental, vision, life & disability coverage, and 401K + employer matching 
  • Voluntary benefits like company-paid identity theft protection and resources for pets, mental health and meditation resources, industry-leading fertility coverage, fully paid leave for childbirth or bonding, fully paid leave for caregivers, programs for loved ones with developmental disabilities and neurodiversity, subsidized back-up child and elder care, and reimbursement for adoption, surrogacy, tuition, and student loans 
  • Investment in your professional growth and development enabling you to thrive in our vibrant community.  
  • The space to accelerate progress, positively disrupt, and create what happens next  
  • Time off for a winter recess 

 

The Orchard is committed to providing equal employment opportunity for all persons regardless of age, disability, national origin, race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity or expression, pregnancy, veteran or military status, marital and civil partnership/union status, alienage or citizenship status, creed, genetic information or any other status protected by applicable federal, state, or local law.

jobs.description:
About Sony Music Entertainment

At Sony Music Entertainment, we fuel the creative journey. We’ve played a pioneering role in music history, from the first-ever music label to the invention of the flat disc record. We’ve nurtured some of music’s most iconic artists and produced some of the most influential recordings of all time.

Today, we work in more than 70 countries, supporting a diverse roster of international superstars, developing and independent artists, and visionary creators. From our position at the intersection of music, entertainment, and technology, we bring imagination and expertise to the newest products and platforms, embrace new business models, employ breakthrough tools, and provide powerful insights that help our artists push creative boundaries and reach new audiences. In everything we do, we’re committed to artistic integrity, transparency, and entrepreneurship.

Sony Music Entertainment is a member of the Sony family of global companies.

 

As a Publicity Director with Alamo and Santa Anna you will work on the publicity strategy and campaigns for our roster of artists across all media formats. The ideal candidate will seamlessly interweave a press narrative and innovative ideas with an artist’s overall marketing campaign. They will display the ability to balance music marketing savvy with attention to detail, follow through and execution on a multi-project level. 

What you'll do:

  • Work with our team to create, implement and manage strategic national publicity campaigns for an evolving roster of artists, including television appearances, print opportunities, digital/web/content opportunities, podcasts, social media takeovers, etc.

  • When applicable, cover television performances/photo shoots/events/award shows and effectively prepare talent and media for interviews and appearances.

  • Prepare clear, insightful reports for executives, external partners, IP stakeholders, and project leaders.

  • Stay ahead of platform updates, cultural moments, and emerging trends, applying strategic judgment to reactive and real-time content opportunities.
  • Creates compelling UGC and organic content from screenings, events, activations, and pop-culture moments.
  • Brings deep fluency in the digital media ecosystem, including KPIs, impressions, CTR, CPMs and engagement metrics.
  • Track competitive activity on an ongoing basis and proactively shares insights with the broader department.
  • Develop clear, effective briefs for external agencies and collaborates on multi-faceted social campaigns.
  • Partner closely with marketing leads to review campaign strategies, maximize cross-platform impact, and ensure timelines, plans, and opportunities are clearly communicated.
  • Track and reports on campaign performance with actionable recommendations for optimization, iteration, and improvement.
  • Build and maintains strong internal and external relationships that encourage collaboration, accountability, and constructive feedback.
  • Translate influencer campaign data into actionable insights that inform strategy, creator selection, and content optimization.
  • Owns and manages the social media calendar, planning and forecasting content weeks to months in advance.

Who you are:

  • Have 4-6 years of hands-on experience developing content for social media accounts + running point on digital campaigns from strategy to execution.
  • Prior experience in entertainment, film/TV, music, or media environments is strongly preferred
  • Proficiency with Adobe Suite programs including Photoshop – to create new visually captivating basic content in a short timeframe
  • Strong experience briefing creative consultants and agencies to produce attractive, effective content, with the ability to guide concept development, oversee execution, and ensure deliverables support broader digital and social campaign strategies.
  • Experience using CapCut and Edits
  • Proven track record working with social media agencies, influencers and external content creators including fluency with negotiation and deadlines
  • Experience with web analytics tools and managing ad platforms in Google Analytics, Meta Business Manager, Tik Tok for Business, etc.
  • Fluency in paid and organic social-understanding how content, media, and platform algorithms work together to drive performance
  • Strategic thinker with a creative POV and clear understanding of what makes content resonate on social media platforms
  • Must be highly organized and detail-oriented, with a strong ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously with un-prompted follow-through
 

What we give you:

  • You join an inclusive, collaborative and global community where you have the opportunity to channel your passion every day  
  • A modern office environment designed to foster productivity, creativity, and teamwork empowering you to bring your best 
  • An attractive and comprehensive benefits package including medical, dental, vision, life & disability coverage, and 401K + employer matching 
  • Voluntary benefits like company-paid identity theft protection and resources for pets, mental health and meditation resources, industry-leading fertility coverage, fully paid leave for childbirth or bonding, fully paid leave for caregivers, programs for loved ones with developmental disabilities and neurodiversity, subsidized back-up child and elder care, and reimbursement for adoption, surrogacy, tuition, and student loans 
  • Investment in your professional growth and development enabling you to thrive in our vibrant community.  
  • The space to accelerate progress, positively disrupt, and create what happens next  
  • Time off for a winter recess

Sony Music is committed to providing equal employment opportunity for all persons regardless of age, disability, national origin, race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity or expression, pregnancy, veteran or military status, marital and civil partnership/union status, alienage or citizenship status, creed, genetic information or any other status protected by applicable federal, state, or local law.

The anticipated annual base salary does not include any other compensation components or other benefits that an individual may be eligible for.  The actual base salary offered depends on a variety of factors, which may include as applicable, the qualifications of the individual applicant for the position, years of relevant experience, specific and unique skills, level of education attained, certifications or other professional licenses held, and the location in which the applicant lives and/or from which they will be performing the job.

$70,000—$75,000 USD

jobs.title: Head of Backstage Marketing
jobs.description:
The Platform team creates the technology that enables Spotify to learn quickly and scale easily, enabling rapid growth in our users and our business around the globe. Spanning many disciplines, we work to make the business work; creating the infrastructure, tooling, frameworks, and capabilities needed to welcome a billion customers.
 
About the Team
Backstage was created at Spotify and has grown into a leading SaaS and open source platform for developer portals. Thousands of companies use Backstage to bring structure to complex microservices environments, improve developer experience, and ship high-quality software faster — including in the age of AI — without losing autonomy or control.Backstage powers centralized software catalogs, standards, and AI enablement capabilities across open source, plugins, and SaaS offerings.
 
Our customers include global enterprises and Fortune 100 companies transforming their engineering culture. This role leads marketing for Shipping Our Expertise (SOE), Spotify’s B2B SaaS business built on our platform capabilities, while also shaping the broader Spotify R&D brand.
